top_jobs.title: Health Data AI/ML Intern
top_jobs.description:
Responsibilities

Join Noblis, a mission-driven nonprofit research organization dedicated to advancing science and technology for the public good.

We are seeking a technical intern for our Noblis 2026 Summer Internship Program.

Position Overview: This internship offers hands‑on experience in AI/ML techniques for data fusion in a complex space. We are looking for a motivated coder to expand their skill sets on a team that will be integrating disparate public health datasets to advance outbreak tracking and prediction.

Responsibilities
• Identify, apply, and adapt the latest research methodologies and open‑source solutions
• Develop and train new machine learning models
• Identify, curate, and process large datasets
• Understand critical metadata and dataset parameters, including for sparse or inconsistent datasets
• Research, apply, analyze, and document technical approaches and their outcomes
• Have opportunities to use various types of compute resources including on premises hardware and cloud resources

Required Qualifications
• A GPA of 3.3 or higher.
• U.S. citizenship
• Demonstrated analytical skills, strong written and oral communications, and collaboration skills.
• Must be actively pursuing an undergraduate or graduate degree with at least one semester remaining before graduation
• Proficiency with data science tools including R/R Shiny, Git, and python

Desired Qualifications
• Experience with or interest in epidemiological and public health data, including genomic, geospatial, and line‑list data
• Familiarity with industry best practices for software/hardware optimization when processing large data sets and experience in the following required task areas: experience with machine learning, statistical modeling, time‑series forecasting, and/or geospatial analytics

Overview

Noblis and our wholly owned subsidiaries, Noblis ESI and Noblis MSD, take on some of the nation’s toughest challenges, delivering advanced solutions to our customers’ most critical missions. We bring together leading scientific, engineering, and management expertise in a culture grounded in objectivity and collaboration, ensuring our work creates lasting impact across federal missions.

We work with a broad range of government agencies in the defense, intelligence, and federal civilian sectors. Learn more and find opportunities at careers.noblis.org

Why Work at Noblis

At Noblis, we share a passion for excellence and innovation, and we create an environment where people can do meaningful work while maintaining the balance that keeps them energized and fulfilled. We seek out individuals with a natural curiosity and desire to collaborate and learn. We believe our people are our greatest strength, and we consistently seek exceptionally skilled, mission‑driven professionals who care deeply about doing work that enriches lives and makes our nation safer.

Noblis has earned numerous workplace awards for our culture, our commitment to employee well‑being, and our dedication to meaningful, impactful work. We also maintain a drug‑free workplace.

Remote/hybrid status is subject to change based on Noblis and/or government requirements.

Commitment to Non-Discrimination

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, ethnicity, sex, age, national origin, religion, physical or mental disability, pregnancy/childbirth and related medical conditions, veteran or military status, or any other characteristics protected by applicable federal, state, or local law.

If reasonable accommodation is needed to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and/or to receive other benefits and privileges of employment, please contact us.

EEO is the Law | E-Verify | Right to Work

Total Rewards

At Noblis we recognize and reward your contributions, provide you with growth opportunities, and support your total well‑being. Our offerings include health, life, disability, financ

top_jobs.title: Data Science Summer Intern
top_jobs.description:
Data Science Internship Summer 2026 – Stamford, CT

Be a part of a revolutionary change!

At PMI, we’ve chosen to do something incredible. We’re totally transforming our business and building our future on one clear purpose – to deliver a smoke-free future.

With huge change, comes huge opportunity. So, wherever you join us, you’ll enjoy the freedom to dream up and deliver better, brighter solutions and the space to move your career forward in endlessly different directions.

Our success depends on people who are committed to our purpose and have an appetite for progress.

Our beautiful HQ in Stamford, CT is just steps away from the Stamford Metro-North Train Station and easily accessible from NYC.

Our 10-week summer internship program provides an opportunity to gain new skills at an organization transforming the industry.  Interns support projects that deliver real impact and provide real value to our organization. Interns participate in various professional development and networking activities throughout the summer.  

The anticipated summer internship start date is June 1, 2026.

The Data Services team is part of US Data Insights and Analytics Organization and is committed to leading the development of cutting-edge data platform and services for PMI & Swedish Match One US. Our focus is on providing engineering services that transform diverse data into well-governed, high-value data assets built on advanced data platforms on the cloud underpinned with security and high quality like Snowflake, AWS, Databricks, Azure based systems. Our mission is to craft innovative solutions that simplify complex data challenges and enable our business users to generate actionable business insights using advanced technologies powered by AI, ML, GenAI, and advanced analytics, establishing us as a premier data services team.

Your ‘day to day’:
• Develop and maintain data pipelines to ingest and transform data from multiple sources for analytics and reporting.
• Assist the Data Modeler with data analysis, profiling, and classification tagging.
• Develop and maintain data products, including Snowflake view development.
• Collaborate with ID&A cross-functional teams, including analytics and reporting, data architects, data engineers, and product owners.
• Identify and implement data quality rules within pipelines to ensure high-quality final data assets.

Who we’re looking for:
• Currently pursuing a degree in Computer Science, Data Science, AI/ML, or related fields during the 2025-2026 school year AND returning to the program after completion of the internship
• Familiarity with ETL tool, Python, SQL and source code mgmt (Jenkins,bitbucket etc.)
• Basic understanding of cloud platforms (e.g., AWS, Azure, or Snowflake).
• Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
• Eagerness to learn, innovate, and contribute to impactful projects.
• Legally authorized to work in the U.S.
• Fluent in English

By the end of the internship, you will:
• Gain hands-one experience building and operationalizing data engineering solutions in enterprise-grade data cloud environments.
• Understand how to apply data engineering solution to address business problems, such as master data management, data governance, integrate workflow and streamline data driven task.
• Learn best practices in data master data management, data governance, integration, data lake, data ingestion and modeling.
• Collaborate with a global team of experts in data engineering.

Hourly Base Salary Range: $32-$35/hr

What we offer:
• We have implemented Smart Work, a hybrid model of working that promotes flexibility in the workplace.
• Seize the freedom to define your future and ours. We’ll empower you to take risks, experiment, and explore.
• Be part of an inclusive, diverse culture where everyone’s contribution is respected; Collaborate with some of the world’s best people and feel like you belong.
• Pursue your ambitions and develop your skills with a global business – our staggering size and

Overview

Join Univest as a Data Analytics / Business Intelligence Intern this summer!

Are you passionate about turning data into actionable insights? Univest Financial Corporation is seeking a curious and motivated intern to join our Data Analytics team this summer in Suderton, PA.

As a Data Analytics / Business Intelligence Intern, you'll work alongside experienced professionals, gaining hands-on experience with real-world data projects, developing dashboards, and presenting your findings to help drive business decisions. • Experience with Power BI, or other visualization tools.
• Advanced Excel knowledge (pivot tables, formulas, charts).
• Strong PowerPoint and communication/presentation skills — able to turn data into stories.
• AI (traditional or generative), LLM, Agentic experience a plus.
• Detail-oriented, curious, and eager to learn.

Salary: $20/hr

top_jobs.title: Data Science Intern, Summer 2026 (Onsite: Winston-Salem, NC)
top_jobs.description:
Discover Your Future with Inmar Intelligence: Where Potential Meets Purpose

Are you ready to do meaningful work that actually makes a difference - not just another summer gig? At Inmar Intelligence, we don't just build technology, we power the modern marketplace. For over 45 years, we've been the trusted partner of some of the world's biggest brands, retailers, and healthcare organizations. From saving consumers billions to making healthcare safer and more efficient, our data-driven solutions shape the way businesses operate.

But what truly sets us apart is our culture. At Inmar, you'll experience a High Challenge, High Support environment that pushes you to grow - without going it alone. We believe in:
• Raising the bar in everything we do
• Seeking truth together, through collaboration and learning
• And putting our Customer First

Here, you won't just be an intern. You'll be part of a team that encourages curiosity, celebrates bold ideas, and gives you the tools to shape your career - starting now.

About this Opportunity

Inmar's Summer Internship Program offers a dynamic and enriching experience designed to give students real-world exposure to a professional environment while contributing meaningfully to company initiatives. Interns will split their time between a high-impact cross-functional team project and department-specific responsibilities assigned by their direct manager.

The MarTech Data Science team contributes to the design, development, and deployment of advanced data science and engineering solutions for Inmar's media and incentives solutions. We are seeking an intern that can bridge data science and engineering to enable scalable, high-performance analytics products and ad-hoc analyses that leverage data for actionable insights. The Data Science Intern will collaborate across multiple teams including data engineering, business intelligence, product, and analytics, to build robust data pipelines, deploy machine learning models, and develop solutions that power predictive analytics. You will have the opportunity (and the expectation!) to leverage enterprise-level AI tools to help you in these projects. If you are the kind of person who gets told a problem is "unsolvable" and your first reaction is "Ok, how would I solve it?", this is the team for you.

Please note: This is an onsite internship based in our Winston-Salem, NC location and will require in-person attendance.

What You'll Do
• Assist in building modular, reusable components for data transformation, feature extraction, and model deployment using modern cloud technologies (Google Cloud Platform).
• Contribute to the design and deployment of machine learning models and advanced analytics pipelines to deliver scalable solutions.
• Collaborate with internal stakeholders on the design of machine learning models and advanced analytics to deliver a range of ad-hoc solutions, with the ability to identify commonalities in requests and propose new products based on recurring themes.

Your Qualifications
• Currently pursuing a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Data Science, Statistics with a concentration in Data Science, Data Engineering, or any related quantitative field with a strong emphasis on statistical programming and working with big data (e.g. Economics, Psychology, Computer Science, etc.).
• Strong analytical, organizational, communication, and problem-solving skills.
• Proficiency in DML (SQL) and cloud systems (Google Cloud preferred, but AWS, Azure, Spark, etc., are all great). You'll be asked to solve complex, novel data problems; we will have a large library of queries, scripts, and routines for you to build off of, but you must be comfortable developing independently from there.
• Proficiency in statistical programming languages and frameworks (e.g., TensorFlow/keras, PyTorch/torch) is required. Python is preferred for production-level code, but R or SAS experience is also great.
• Proficiency in using AI for code enhancement and problem-solv

We are Lenovo. We do what we say. We own what we do. We WOW our customers.

Lenovo is a US$69 billion revenue global technology powerhouse, ranked #196 in the Fortune Global 500, and serving millions of customers every day in 180 markets. Focused on a bold vision to deliver Smarter Technology for All, Lenovo has built on its success as the world’s largest PC company with a full-stack portfolio of AI-enabled, AI-ready, and AI-optimized devices (PCs, workstations, smartphones, tablets), infrastructure (server, storage, edge, high performance computing and software defined infrastructure), software, solutions, and services.

Lenovo’s continued investment in world-changing innovation is building a more equitable, trustworthy, and smarter future for everyone, everywhere.

This transformation together with Lenovo’s world-changing innovation is building a more inclusive, trustworthy, and smarter future for everyone, everywhere. To find out more visit www.lenovo.com, and read about the latest news via our StoryHub.

Lenovo is a global technology powerhouse, delivering smarter technology for all. As the Official Technology Partner of the FIFA World Cup 2026™ this summer, Lenovo is helping power one of the world’s biggest sporting events through innovative devices, services, and AI-driven solutions.
About the Role

Lenovo is a global leader in innovative technology, and our Global Social Media Team drives strategic paid media and analytics initiatives worldwide. • Basic proficiency in Python and/or Javascript for scripting and API integrations, and SQL for data querying and manipulation.

• Experience with agentic AI automation and orchestration, especially with autonomous workflows.
• Experience with AI prompt structuring and validation.
• Strong analytical mindset with the ability to handle data structures and perform basic transformations.
• Excellent communication skills and a collaborative team player.
• Authorization to work in [relevant country] without sponsorship.
Preferred Skills
• Experience with API connections or automation projects (e.g., via personal or academic work).
• Knowledge

top_jobs.title: IT Business Analyst Intern (Summer 2026)
top_jobs.description:
JOB DESCRIPTION

About the Opportunity
As a participant in the Co-op/Intern Professional Development Program, you will be cultivating professional skills and performing professional level work at FirstEnergy. Each participant will receive on-the-job training, and challenging assignments to refine skills and round out competencies. Summer Internships with FirstEnergy are full-time opportunities spanning 14 weeks. FirstEnergy offers paid internships to qualified students actively enrolled in accredited university or college. As an IT Business Analyst Intern at FirstEnergy, you will be on an IT team tasked to implement or maintain technology used to run an area of our corporate business. The role of an IT business analyst is to be the link between the technology and the business objectives. The business analyst listens, asks questions, analyzes and then documents what the team is describing they need. The business analyst knows exactly what is needed; therefore, the business analyst is critical to the successful testing and implementation of the technology. In this position, you will not be writing code. This position is for the analytical thinker who loves learning business processes and new technology. This position will be in the IT Corporate Products team and support corporate technology functions such as Finance, Physical Security, Flights Operations, and Compliance. Note: The opportunity is on-site and will work in-person four days/week. As such, you must be local to the Cleveland/Akron/Canton (Ohio) area
In this position, you will have the opportunity to:
Learn the business processes that you and your team are supporting. Learn the technology that supports the business processes. Work with your team gathering requirements for technology changes or implementations. Learn the business requirements so that you can develop or modify functional specifications. Complete unit, integration and regression testing for application projects or upgrades, ensuring that correct business functionality is being delivered. Use query tools such as TOAD, Power BI, and QlikView. Collaborate directly with the FirstEnergy business units, as well as other departments of IT, to complete and/or manage your assignments. Deliver effective client support through the investigation and resolution of client issues. Learn how to adhere to FirstEnergy Sarbanes Oxley (SOX) Controls.
Applicant Requirements
Currently enrolled in a bachelor's degree program at an accredited university/college with a major in Management Information Systems, Information Systems Management, Business degree with a minor in a technology program, or an equivalent degree. Class Standing - Junior (graduation date of December 2026 / May 2027) or Sophomore (graduation date of December 2027 / May 2028). Graduation date MUST be listed on resume Maintain a minimum cumulative GPA of
2.5. remaining_jobs.title: Intern, Business Analyst Eastern Region Summer 2026
remaining_jobs.description:
As an independent group of companies, the BMW Group has a commitment to creativity and breakthrough ideas that goes well beyond the racetrack. In order to continuously create ultimate driving machines, we drive our growth and design excellence by staffing our teams with individuals who are innovative and always looking for the next great idea. If you share our vision and view yourself as an independent, creative thinker, we invite you to join our team in this exceptional role located in Woodcliff Lake, NJ.

Summer 2026 Full-Time Internship – June 1, 2026 – August 21, 2026

WHAT AWAITS YOU.
• Research, data mine, and prepare reports for various stakeholders.
• Participate in the preparation of business presentations for various internal meetings, including Market and Business Reviews and Marketing Meetings.
• Assist with validating the accuracy and integrity of data used in reports and presentations.
• Help with managing communications across different departments to ensure data integrity as well as timely delivery of required presentations.
• Contribute to ideas to improve reporting processes, automation, or data visualization techniques.

WHAT YOU SHOULD BRING.
• Currently enrolled in a bachelor or master’s degree program at an accredited college or university 
• Possess a minimum cumulative GPA of 3.0 (not just in major)
• Have completed at least 60 college credits or be at least a rising Junior
• Field of study: Communications or Business
• Must attach a copy of college unofficial transcript
• Ability to work part-time (20-29 hours/week)
• Prior BMW Group experience (applicable to international J1 students only)
• Demonstrated strong on or off-campus leadership roleExperience in tracking progress/performance and analyzing trends to determine what areas of improvement are needed
• Proven problem solver, capable of trouble shooting issues and finding ways to improve effectiveness
• Demonstrated strong ability to focus on customer experience via management of various stakeholders

WHAT YOU CAN LOOK FORWARD TO.
• Medical Insurance
• Paid Time Off in addition to Company paid holidays where eligible
• Hybrid work environment
• Intern Vehicle Lease Program

This is a part-time internship position for our spring rotation, from January 12, 2026 through May 15, 2026 in Woodcliff Lake, NJ.

The hourly rate for Undergraduate students is $25.30

The hourly rate for Graduate students is $32.20

The selected student's academic level will be used to determine the final pay rate. This statement is in accordance with state and local pay disclosure requirements.

As part of the hiring process, you may be required to successfully pass a background check. A satisfactory completion of a background investigation including verification of education, prior employment, criminal history, credit check history, and pre-employment drug screen to the extent permissible under applicable state law, is a condition of your offer of employment and your continued employment. The results of your background investigation are satisfactory and acceptable in the sole judgement and discretion of BMW Shared Services, LLC.

Even more so than the generous compensation and benefits, the culture and values of BMW of North America makes it the ultimate working environment. These values are Responsibility, Appreciation, Transparency, Trust, and Openness. We allow these values to guide the way we conduct ourselves and our business.

BMW in the United States is an equal opportunity employer. It is the policy of BMW Group in the United States to provide equal employment opportunity (EEO) to all qualified persons regardless of age, race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability or veteran status.

remaining_jobs.title: 2026 Canon Insights Summer Internship - Audit & Business Consultation Intern
remaining_jobs.description:
About the Role

2026 Canon Insights Summer Internship

Canon, U. S. A., Inc. is currently hiring for our 2026 Canon Insights Internship Program.

The program will be held from Monday, June 1, 2026 - Wednesday, August 5, 2026.

Canon is an innovative global digital imaging leader that uses technology to dissolve the barriers we
face in the connections we build, the experiences we create, and even the lives we live. We are
looking for passionate and experienced students to join the Canon USA team. This is a paid summer
internship 10-week robust experience based in our Melville, New York Headquarters.

What You'll Do:
- You will have the opportunity to gain hands-on work experience by participating in significant
work projects that help you learn, develop business skills, and provide a meaningful
contribution to Canon's business and goals.
- You will be exposed to world-class teams and our business, marketing, and strategy initiatives
through training, meetings, presentations, and other planned activities.
- You'll gain marketable skills and knowledge that you'll need in transitioning into the workforce.
- You'll come to the office 3 days a week and 2 days with the option to work from home (unless
a specific business need arises requiring in-office attendance on other days). This position
offers a hybrid work schedule on full-time (9 AM - 5 PM) schedule based on a 35-hour work
week. Note that work schedules and office reporting requirements may change from time to
time based on business needs.
Perks & Benefits:
- A beautiful Headquarters facility, which has consistently received awards for its many modern
amenities on a 52-acre campus.
- Free coffee.
- "Dress for Your Day" attire program - Casual dress, including jeans is permitted!

- Opportunity to network with top-level Canon business leaders.
- Exclusive training with industry leaders on Canon equipment.
- Volunteer opportunities serving our local community.
- Opportunity to participate in exclusive focus groups.
- A chance to be featured on Canon's social media sites.
- #CanonIntern Swag!
Award-Winning Culture:
Canon has a unique culture and is committed to our core values of integrity, mutual respect, fairness,
and communication. Our corporate philosophy is Kyosei - which we define as "all people, regardless
of race, religion, or culture, harmoniously living and working together into the future." This is reflected
in our welcoming, respectful, and family-oriented atmosphere with a strong focus on diversity &
inclusion, work-life balance, and community and environmental initiatives.

Your Impact

The 2026 Canon Insights Summer Internship - Audit & Business Consultation Intern will be responsible for the following:

- Participate in attending meetings with various members of the ABC group.
- Participate in performing internal control testing.
- Participate in helping to conduct research on technical accounting matters as they arise.
- Participate in assisting the business analysts with data analytics and visualizations.
- Participate in assisting with special projects as they arise via requests from management.

About You: The Skills & Expertise You Bring

An enrolled undergraduate student who is currently a junior and entering their senior year as of Summer 2026, pursuing a Bachelor's degree aligned with the department of interest. A strong communicator-both written and verbal-with advanced proficiency in Google Workspace (Sheets, Docs, Slides, Chat, Calendar and Drive). Brings the ability to thrive as a collaborative team player while also working independently with confidence and enthusiasm.

- Accounting major preferred.
- Experience with data visualization and coding platforms such as Tableau, Power BI, Python, SQL also preferred.
- Team player and work independently.

remaining_jobs.title: Data Science Intern; Graduate Student - Summer
remaining_jobs.description:
Position: Data Science Intern (Graduate Student) - Summer 2026

A little about us:

The Red Alpha Data Science practice grew out of Red Alpha's reputation in Software and System Engineering with our Department of Defense clients. As sometimes happens, customers who trust our expertise in adjacent areas asked Red Alpha to assist with some of their burgeoning Data Science problems.

Culturally, it probably suffices to say that we take our work seriously, but not ourselves. Our leaders have spent time in the trenches and have cursed daylight savings time changes and trailing whitespace as many times as you have. We like to say that we spend 80% of our time cleaning the data...and 20% of our time complaining about cleaning the data.

Joking aside, our voices matter, and it is easy to see how our decisions affect the Data Science practice and Red Alpha as a whole. We have a clear vision of where we are headed.

Our team takes a pragmatic approach to Data Science, defining it loosely as the intersection of technical expertise, business acumen, and soft skills to solve business problems with data. We spend a lot of time trying to understand the problem before we set about building a solution, and we prefer lower tech useful solutions over shiny algorithms and dust on the shelf.

Did we mention we’re pragmatic? We have a diverse set of skills across our team, and whether you are a traditional Data Scientist (whatever that means…), an Applied Research Mathematician, a Database Engineer, a Full Stack Developer, or something else in that neighborhood, if you have a knack for picking apart data to make sense of it, we would enjoy having a conversation with you.
A

day in the life:

The Red Alpha Data Science Internship (summer of 2025) is a thorough and pointed experience doing real-time, meaningful data science work. Throughout your time at Red Alpha, you will take on tough problems, get executive exposure, engage in out-of-the-box solutions development, and participate in technical challenges. There are no gophers here. You will be treated as a professional peer, because you are one!

Red Alpha invests heavily in the training, education, and professional mentorship of our interns. Each intern is paired with a mentor during their time with RED ALPHA to ensure they are engaged and immersed in work and experiences that will help them to grow professionally and broaden their technical tool set. From technology to business, we are looking for people who can innovate, collaborate and drive Red Alpha into the next era of an already rapidly – evolving digital marketplace.

You must be willing to live and work full‑time in the Columbia, MD area for the duration of the internship.

Work Hard, Play Hard, Succeed Together! That's Red Alpha. Join Us!
What you bring to the table:

Now on to the fun of formal requirements - we have to apologize in advance for the corporate-speak here, but just hold your breath for a few lines and everything will be okay. We are actively hiring data professionals for roles across a broad range of skill levels and projects. Our goal is to find the best fit for you so please note that if you apply to one of them and we see a fit elsewhere we will let you know.

So do not worry about applying initially for every position you might be interested in.
• You must be enrolled in a graduate degree program and majoring in Computer Science, Data Science, Computer Systems or a related technical discipline at an accredited higher learning institution.
• Experience with Python, Java, C++, R, and/or other related programming languages is required.
• Must have completed at least six (6) months of prior internship experience.
• Due to the nature of our work, customers we support and the requirements surrounding our office space, you must be eligible to obtain a TS/SCI with Polygraph level of clearance.
The total package:

Our total compensation package was strategically designed with our members in mind with the intention to: reward ou

remaining_jobs.title: Geospatial Technology Analyst Intern (Summer 2026)
remaining_jobs.description:
JOB DESCRIPTION

FirstEnergy at a Glance
We are a forward-thinking electric utility powered by a diverse team of employees committed to making customers' lives brighter, the environment better and our communities stronger. FirstEnergy (NYSE: FE) is dedicated to integrity, safety, reliability and operational excellence. Headquartered in Akron, Ohio, FirstEnergy includes one of the nation's largest investor-owned electric systems, more than 24,000 miles of transmission lines that connect the Midwest and Mid-Atlantic regions, and a regulated generating fleet with a total capacity of more than 3,500 megawatts.
About the Opportunity
This is an open position with FirstEnergy Service Company, a subsidiary of FirstEnergy Corp. [SC00] As a participant in the Co-op/Intern Professional Development Program, you will be cultivating professional skills and performing professional level work. You won't just be sitting in meetings and shadowing all summer; you'll be actively contributing and doing the same work as other FirstEnergy employees on your team. You'll receive on-the-job training and challenging assignments. You will also be assigned a Supervisor, a Coach/Mentor and a dedicated IT Intern Program Coordinator to help you maximize your learning and growth throughout your summer... and beyond. Internships with FirstEnergy are full-time, paid opportunities spanning 14 weeks. You must be actively enrolled in an accredited university or college.
About the Geospatial Technology Analyst
The Geospatial Technology Analyst works with a team of analysts, administrators and architects in support of all things Geospatial related to Distribution (Dx), Transmission (Tx) and Vegetation Management (VMS) and other related applications. These systems empower designers, mappers, & third-party contractors for safe and reliable energy delivery. Team members apply software, hardware, networking, and/or engineering principles to support FirstEnergy's environment. The work focuses on maintaining a system architecture that meets the end-user requirements, while maintaining compliance with industry standards and regulatory requirements. Note: This opportunity is on-site and will work in-person four days/week. Position may be filled at Akron, OH; Greensburg, PA; Reading, PA; or Fairmont, WV locations.
Examples of possible job duties and responsibilities of an Geospatial Technology Analyst:
Understanding the business functions to provide optimal solutions that are scalable, reliable, and cost effective. Managing the testing and deployment of patches and software upgrades. Maintaining integrations and interfaces between FirstEnergy systems Performing system security administration tasks Supporting GSI DDS & ESRI ArcGIS for Designing and Mapping interfaces Supporting data extraction and transformation through the FME Workbench and Python scripts Developing data queries and extracts to meet customer requirements built on Oracle DB and Microsoft SQL Server Technologies Developing a solid understanding of processes in the FirstEnergy Operational Technology line of business, keeping up with developments in the electric utility industry at large, and being able to speak the language of the business.
Applicant Requirements:
Currently enrolled, full time, in a bachelor's degree program at an accredited university/college focusing on Computer Science, Computer Information Systems, Computer Engineering or other technology related disciplines Class Standing - Rising Junior or Rising Senior Maintain a minimum cumulative GPA of 2.5 Must have the ability to work without sponsorship (now or in the future)
Preferred Skills/Experience Basic understanding of, or experience with, any kind of coding or scripting language (e.g. Perl, Python, Java, C#, PowerShell, SQL etc.) Experience with relational databases, including architecture, design, and reading and revising data through software applications Experience supporting software solutions Experience troubleshooting personal devices,
remaining_jobs.match_reason: Geospatial analytics role at major utility company with potential data analysis components, but niche domain (geospatial technology) outside candidate's core analytics experience.

remaining_jobs.title: Summer Intern – Community Intelligence & Growth (Legion Gaming)
remaining_jobs.description:
Description and Requirements

About Lenovo & Legion

Lenovo is the world’s largest PC company, powering innovation for millions of customers globally. Within Lenovo, Legion and LOQ are our high-performance sibling gaming brands; together ranked #1 in the world in the gaming PC category, built for gamers who push boundaries and reach their impossible.

About the Role

We are looking for a highly analytical and curious intern to help us build a next-generation Community Intelligence Engine for Legion’s global gaming community.

This role sits at the intersection of data, marketing and strategy. You will work on a high-impact project focused on understanding and improving the health of our communities across platforms such as Instagram, TikTok, YouTube, Twitch, and Facebook. Need to go beyond just reporting. You will be expected to turn data into insights, and insights into action.

What You’ll Work On

You will design and build a system that helps answer:
• Is our community getting stronger or weaker over time?
• What type of content drives meaningful engagement and growth?
• What should we do differently to improve community outcomes?

Your work will include:
• Defining a Community Health Framework (beyond vanity metrics)
• Aggregating and analyzing cross-platform social media data
• Identifying key drivers of engagement, growth, and retention
• Building a dashboard to track community health on an ongoing basis
• Translating insights into a clear, actionable growth playbook

Key Responsibilities
• Develop a structured framework to measure community health across growth, engagement, and advocacy
• Analyze content performance across formats, themes, and platforms
• Identify patterns that explain what works, what doesn’t, and why
• Build dashboards (Power BI/Tableau) for ongoing tracking and decision-making
• Deliver clear recommendations to improve engagement and community growth
• Present findings in a concise, business-oriented format

Stretch Opportunity

For candidates interested in advanced analytics:
• Build predictive models to forecast content performance
• Identify early signals of declining engagement or community fatigue

What We’re Looking For
• Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
• Experience with data analysis tools (Python, Excel, SQL, or similar)
• Familiarity with data visualization tools (Power BI, Tableau, etc.)
• Understanding of social media platforms and digital content dynamics
• Ability to translate data into clear, actionable insights
• Strong communication and storytelling skills

What You’ll Gain
• Hands-on experience solving a real-world growth problem
• Exposure to global gaming and digital community strategy
• Opportunity to build a high-impact, end-to-end project
• Mentorship and visibility with cross-functional teams

Success in This Role Looks Like

By the end of the internship, you will have built a system that:
• Clearly measures community health across platforms
• Identifies what drives engagement and growth
• Enables the team to make better, faster content decisions

Guiding Principle

We’re looking for insight-driven action. If you’re excited about combining data, creativity, and strategy to solve real business problems—and want to help gamers reach their impossible—we’d love to hear from you.

• Support project wrap-ups and assist with the launch of new projects throughout the year.

• Assisting with group inbox management.
• Provide support internally within the company, while learning how various Subdivisions work as one.

Career Area:

Students and Graduates

Job Description:

Your Work Shapes the World at Caterpillar Inc.

When you join Caterpillar, you're joining a global team who cares not just about the work we do – but also about each other. We are the makers, problem solvers, and future world builders who are creating stronger, more sustainable communities. We don't just talk about progress and innovation here – we make it happen, with our customers, where we work and live. Together, we are building a better world, so we can all enjoy living in it.

Job Summary

Caterpillar offers a unique opportunity within our Business Operations organization to allow students to gain a wealth of real-world practical experience and establish core competencies. Business Operations professionals across the enterprise help our dealers and customers succeed in their business through the promotion and selling of products and services, including market research and advertising to the industries we serve. Business Operations Interns help define and deliver premium and consistent online experiences that generate leads, sales, and customer loyalty by accelerating transformational innovation and aggressively developing and growing Caterpillar’s customer-facing digital business and services, revenue, and profit. Business, Marketing, Analytics, Communications degrees or similar preferred.

Specialties within Business Operations include but are not limited to: 
• Channel Development
• Account Management
• Equipment & Services Sales Management
• Sales Learning
• Site Application Performance & Technology
• Business Development/ Strategy
• Sales, Operations & Support
• Commercialization

Additional Info:
• This is a full- time (40 hours per week) 12-week internship May 18, 2026 – August 7, 2026 (subject to change based on selected candidates schedule).
• Locations for this internship could be Peoria, IL; Cary, NC; Houston, TX; Seguin, TX; Irving, TX; Tucson, AZ or other locations throughout the US

What You Will Do:
• Build foundational knowledge of Caterpillar, its products, services and customers.
• Grow personally and professionally through soft skills development and hands-on technical training.
• Collaborate with experts in product development, dealer management, marketing, and sales, etc.
• Work in various rotational assignments throughout Caterpillar, learning strategy, services and products
• Ultimately, manage assigned territory and serve as a connection between dealers and Caterpillar.
